name: Nsc (Naca Counseling Subsidiary) - Baltimore, Md
description_short: NACA is a nonprofit housing advocacy organization offering character-based mortgage lending and HUD-approved housing counseling to underserved communities without credit score consideration.
description_long:
Founded in 1988 by the Boston Hotel Workers Union, the Neighborhood Assistance Corporation of America (NACA) has spent 36 years challenging predatory lending practices and expanding homeownership access for low- to moderate-income households and communities of color. The organization operates as both a nonprofit advocate and a mortgage lender, with a subsidiary counseling service based in Baltimore. NACA's core mission centers on closing the racial wealth disparity gap through character-based lending that prioritizes borrower circumstances over credit scores.

NACA's primary offerings include their signature mortgage product featuring no down payment, no closing costs or fees, no mortgage insurance, and fixed rates below market average (currently 5.625% for 30-year loans as of April 2026). The organization also provides HUD-approved housing counseling—accounting for 30% of all HUD housing counseling nationally—and operates "Achieve the Dream" events in multiple cities where prospective members can access services and become NACA-qualified. Members gain access to a digital portal for file management, document uploads, and status tracking. The organization has served 3 million people, helped 500,000 become homeowners, issued 75,000 mortgages, and secured $20 billion in mortgage commitments.

NACA distinguishes itself through its character-based lending model that ignores credit scores entirely, its aggressive advocacy against predatory landlords and corporate real estate investors, and its volunteer-driven community engagement structure. The organization maintains partnerships with lenders, servicers, investors, contractors, and real estate professionals. Their emphasis on activism and member participation—through the "NACtivist" volunteer program—reflects a social justice orientation uncommon among traditional mortgage lenders. NACA also addresses affordable housing through initiatives like affordable modular homes in Selma.

The main caveat is that NACA's services require active participation and membership qualification through their events or processes, meaning access isn't immediate or online-only. As a nonprofit with a specific mission, their mortgage offerings may have additional qualification requirements beyond credit scores, though the website does not detail these prerequisites. While the organization's track record is substantial, borrowers should verify current rates, terms, and eligibility criteria directly.
pros: No credit score consideration—character-based lending evaluates circumstances instead
No down payment, no closing costs, no fees, and no mortgage insurance requirements
Below-market fixed rates (5.625% for 30-year as of April 2026) with multiple term options
HUD-approved housing counseling provided directly (30% of all national HUD counseling)
Established track record: 500,000 homeowners helped, $20B in mortgage commitments
Free in-person events (Achieve the Dream) in multiple cities for qualification and member onboarding
Member portal for secure document upload and case status tracking
cons: Services require attending multi-day in-person events in specific cities or membership qualification process—not immediately accessible online
Limited geographic reach initially; events only occur in select cities (Hartford, Little Rock, Milwaukee, Fort Myers, St. Louis)
Website does not specify all eligibility requirements or income limits, requiring direct inquiry
As a nonprofit with advocacy focus, mortgage volume and capacity may be limited compared to traditional lenders
No information provided about approval timelines or specific mortgage terms beyond rates
best_for: First-time homebuyers with low-to-moderate income and poor or nonexistent credit history
Communities of color and historically underserved populations seeking fair lending practices
Buyers seeking to avoid predatory lending and aligned with NACA's social justice mission
People unable to save for down payments or closing costs
diagnosis: NACA is best for low-to-moderate income first-time homebuyers and communities of color who prioritize fair lending practices and can participate in their qualification events. The main caveat is that accessing services requires attending multi-day regional events and completing membership qualification—this is not an online-only lender and prospective borrowers must proactively engage with the organization's process.
services: Character-based mortgage lending without credit score consideration
Fixed-rate mortgages with no down payment requirement
Mortgages with zero closing costs and zero fees
Mortgages with no private mortgage insurance
HUD-approved housing counseling (represents 30% of national HUD counseling)
Multi-day 'Achieve the Dream' events for membership qualification and onboarding
Member portal for document management and status tracking
Volunteer program (NACtivist) for community engagement and event participation
Advocacy against predatory landlords and corporate real estate investors
Partnership facilitation with lenders, servicers, real estate agents, and contractors
Podcasts and educational content on homeownership barriers
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 11 E Mount Royal Ave, Suite 101, Baltimore, MD 21202
meta_description: NACA provides character-based mortgage lending and HUD-approved housing counseling without credit score requirements.
